questions.question: Who are Sue and Johnsy?
questions.answer: Sue and Johnsy are two young artists who share a small flat.

questions.question: Where did Sue and Johnsy live?
questions.answer: They lived in a small flat on the third storey of an old house.

questions.question: What illness did Johnsy suffer from?
questions.answer: Johnsy suffered from pneumonia.

questions.question: Why was Sue worried about Johnsy?
questions.answer: Sue was worried because Johnsy had lost the will to live.

questions.question: What did Johnsy do while lying in bed?
questions.answer: She looked out of the window and counted the falling ivy leaves.

questions.question: What strange belief did Johnsy have about the leaves?
questions.answer: She believed she would die when the last leaf fell.

questions.question: What plant grew on the wall outside the window?
questions.answer: An ivy creeper grew on the wall.

questions.question: Who was Behrman?
questions.answer: Behrman was an old painter who lived in the same building.

questions.question: What was Behrman’s dream?
questions.answer: His dream was to paint a masterpiece.

questions.question: Why did Sue call Behrman?
questions.answer: She called him to be a model for her painting.

questions.question: How did Behrman react to Johnsy’s belief?
questions.answer: He called her foolish and became angry.

questions.question: What was the weather like during the night of the storm?
questions.answer: It was stormy with heavy rain and strong winds.

questions.question: What did Johnsy see the next morning?
questions.answer: She saw that the last leaf was still on the creeper.

questions.question: What effect did the leaf have on Johnsy?
questions.answer: It gave her hope and the will to live.

questions.question: What realization did Johnsy have later?
questions.answer: She realized it was wrong to wish for death.

questions.question: What did Sue give Johnsy when she started recovering?
questions.answer: She gave her hot soup and a mirror.

questions.question: What did Johnsy do after getting better?
questions.answer: She combed her hair and smiled.

questions.question: What did the doctor say about Johnsy’s recovery?
questions.answer: He said she would soon recover because she now had the will to live.

questions.question: What happened to Behrman?
questions.answer: Behrman caught pneumonia and died.

questions.question: What items were found in Behrman’s room?
questions.answer: A ladder, a lantern, and paints were found.

questions.question: Why did Behrman go out in the storm?
questions.answer: He went out to paint the last leaf on the wall.

questions.question: Why is the painted leaf called Behrman’s masterpiece?
questions.answer: It saved Johnsy’s life by giving her hope.

questions.question: What quality of Sue is shown in the story?
questions.answer: Sue’s kindness and true friendship are shown.

questions.question: What message does the story convey?
questions.answer: Hope and selfless sacrifice can save lives.

questions.question: Why is the title 'The Last Leaf' important?
questions.answer: It represents hope and the sacrifice that saved Johnsy.
